title: Emesent Launches GX1 — All-in-One SLAM LiDAR, RTK and 360° Imagery Scanner
excerpt: Unveiled at Geo Week 2026, the GX1 integrates SLAM mapping, RTK positioning, and 360° imaging into a single handheld unit — designed for GPS-denied environments.

title: RIEGL Debuts VUX-820-G — Topographic and Bathymetric LiDAR in One Drone Payload
excerpt: The 5.7kg VUX-820-G maps land and shallow water in a single drone flight, with embedded GNSS/IMU for 2–3cm accuracy. Shown at INTERGEO 2025.

title: Topcon Expands Capture Reality Portfolio with New Handheld Scanner
excerpt: Topcon introduced a portable handheld scanning system targeting field crews who need fast reality capture without tripod setups.

title: New Leica MultiMapper Brings Portability and Versatility to Airborne Mapping
excerpt: Leica's MultiMapper targets light aircraft and large UAV operators needing flexible airborne mapping — bridging the gap between drone payloads and high-end scanners like the CityMapper.

title: Revolution Geosystems Acquires Phoenix LiDAR Systems
excerpt: The acquisition accelerates consolidation in the UAV LiDAR market as demand for integrated end-to-end geospatial workflows continues to grow.

title: Esri and Pix4D Launch Real-Time Terrestrial Mapping Workflow
excerpt: Field crews can now capture photogrammetry data with Pix4D and visualize results in ArcGIS in near real time — collapsing the traditional days-long data delivery gap.

title: Geo Week 2026 Draws 3,500 Professionals — Workforce Gap Dominates Conversations
excerpt: Denver hosted 238 exhibitors from 25 countries. AI-assisted workflows and drone-ground tradeoffs dominated the show floor. A structural talent shortage is the industry's quiet crisis.

title: Gaussian Splatting Goes Mainstream — ArcGIS Native Support Signals Tipping Point
excerpt: Gaussian Splatting — photorealistic 3D from images — is now natively supported in ArcGIS. Shrinking file sizes and faster rendering are driving rapid adoption in professional GIS workflows.

title: Mach9 Unveils AI-Assisted Digital Surveyor — Human Control Stays Central
excerpt: Mach9 redesigned its AI workflow to enable real-time collaboration between surveyors and AI — accepting suggestions interactively rather than reviewing fully automated output.

title: Topcon Unveils Machine Control and Geomatics Innovations at CONEXPO 2026
excerpt: Updates across GNSS receivers, robotic total stations, and machine control systems were shown at CONEXPO in Las Vegas, with a focus on construction survey workflows.

title: IDS GeoRadar Launches MyMO — Portable Non-Contact Structural Monitor
excerpt: The MyMO uses radar interferometry to detect sub-millimeter displacement in bridges, walls, and slopes — without attaching sensors to the structure.

title: DJI Zenmuse L2 vs L1 — What the Upgrade Actually Gets You in the Field
excerpt: The L2 delivers 240k pts/sec and 450m range vs the L1's lower specs — but for most projects under 120m AGL in open terrain, the cost difference may not justify the upgrade.
